nexacro X-API for JAVA

X-API is an API for communication with nexacro platform client.

See: Description

Packages 
Package Description
com.nexacro.java.xapi.data
Define X-API Data Structure.
com.nexacro.java.xapi.data.datatype  
com.nexacro.java.xapi.tx
Conduct X-API Data Communication.
com.nexacro.java.xapi.tx.impl  

X-API is an API for communication with nexacro platform client.

1. Introduction

Main purpose of the X-API is to provide communication with nexacro platform client, data transmit and receive, or to simplify operation of transmitted/received data to allow developer to focus on business development.

Data transmitted/received between client and server exists as singular data consisted of key-value and as two-dimensional data similar to the Table in DB. It is to operate or transmit/receive such data.

Refer to com.nexacro.java.xapi.data and com.nexacro.java.xapi.tx for major function of X-API.

2. Installation

To install X-API, simply include jar file to class path. Include in class path defined while driving JVM or in /WEB-INF/lib directory of Web Application.

Following are X-API jar files.

File Name Description Necessity
nexacro-xapi-x.x.x.jar X-API yes

X-API require higher than JDK or JRE 1.4, and external component to use X-API is as below.

Component Name Description Necessity Reference
Apache Commons Logging X-API internal logging yes http://jakarta.apache.org/commons/logging/
json-simple X-API internal yes https://code.google.com/archive/p/json-simple/

3. License

The file name of the license for X-APIs is "nexacro_server_license.xml". You must gain this file. Without the license, X-APIs will not function properly. The license is issued by the customer support center.

License file should be located in the same directory as jar file or included in class path. Preferentially load license file located in the same directory as jar file, and if it is not in the same location, load license file in class path. In case of including in class path, locate in /WEB-INF/classes of Web Application.

4. Version

X-API version is included in jar file name as below.

  • nexacro-xapi-<version>.jar

For more information, execute as below. Following information will be helpful for X-API customer support.

C:\>java -jar nexacro-xapi-1.0.0.jar

--- Manifest ---
Manifest-Version: 1.0
Ant-Version: Apache Ant 1.7.0
Created-By: 1.5.0_16-b02 (Sun Microsystems Inc.)
Built-By: Web2.0 Team
Built-Date: February 18 2009
Main-Class: com.nexacro.java.xapi.util.JarInfo

Name: com.nexacro.java.xapi
Specification-Title: X-API
Specification-Vendor: NEXAWEB
Specification-Version: 1.0
Implementation-Title: X-API
Implementation-Vendor-Id: com.nexacro
Implementation-Vendor: NEXAWEB
Implementation-Version: 1.0.0.360
Output Example

5. Installation Verification

In order to verify that X-API is properly installed, compose and execute JSP file as below. Output content includes output information as well as license information.

<%@ page contentType="text/html; charset=UTF-8" %>

<html>
<head>
<title>JarInfo</title>
<style>
* { font-family: Verdana }
</style>
</head>
<body>
<pre>
<%
new com.nexacro.java.xapi.util.JarInfo().info(out);
%>
</pre>
</body>
</html>
Installation Verification JSP
